utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm dr. evelyn , one of the kidney doctors . it's good to meet you guys .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's nice to meet you also .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . so i was reading about this syndrome that i actually have never heard of .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , me too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i do n't think it's very common .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: definitely not . it's c- pretty rare .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: can you start at the beginning ? i know she's a twin , so are these your first two babies ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i have a son also who is nine . he also has autism .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and when the twins were born , katherine , she was about 4 pounds , 8 ounces . and her twin was a bit smaller , at 3 pounds , 13 ounces .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: katherine , she was doing fine . she just had problems with eating , where she would stop breathing when she was eating .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: like preemie type stuff ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh- . yeah . she just had a hard time regulating her temperature , but she did fine . she does have a gi doctor , because she has reflex really bad . she also had a dietician , who told us to take her off cow's milk . which we did . and then she has seen an allergist , and also a neurologist ... who diagnosed her with this syndrome , because she still does n't walk and she was n't sitting by herself a year old .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but so now she is crawling and she is trying to take steps , so think she's doing pretty good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . is she in therapy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: she is in therapy . she's in feeding therapy , occupational therapy , and also physical therapy .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: awesome . okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and we also have speech therapy , who is going to be starting within the next couple of weeks .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , she has a lot of therapies . we have also seen an orthopedic and an ophthalmologist . i can never say that . we have seen everything , really .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and audiology too , right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , wow. .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it has definitely been a whirlwind of stuff . when we saw the geneticist , she told us that sometimes people with this syndrome , they have trouble with their kidneys . that they might actually fuse into one . she also said sometimes they have problems with their legs , so that was why we saw ortho .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so we have seen everybody , really . we are just here to make sure that her kidneys are looking good right now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , okay . so , um , tell me about how many wet diapers she has in a 24 hour period ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: she has a lot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so like normal 8 to 10 , or like 20 ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it's around 8 to 10 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , she seems to pee a lot , and it feels like she drinks a lot too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's perfect .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and she used to only drink milk , and then i took her off dairy milk . so when i say milk , i actually mean , you know , ripple pea protein milk .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure , yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i give her that milk , water now that she's used to it , and sometimes water with just a little bit of juice . so i do feel like she's drinking a lot better now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's great . and she's how old now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: she'll be two mo- two next month .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . is her twin a boy or a girl ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: she's a girl .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and how's she doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: she's doing really good . she's running around , and she does n't have any problems .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . is she bigger than her or the same size ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: they're about the same size . they're able to wear the same clothes , so ...

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't even think she's a pound hav- heavier , actually .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but she is a little bit taller than her ... um , katherine . she's just sh- a little shorter and chunkier , but i think that's a part of her syndrome .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . yeah , i was reading all the things associated with the syndrome . it sounds like we're looking for continual- congenital anomalies wi- of the kidney and urinary tract . which is basically something is wrong with the plumbing .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so the only way to know that , is to do a kidney ultrasound .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . let me put that into the system , and then downstairs they can do the ultrasound .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right , thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , yeah . where do you all live ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , we live in dallas .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . anybody in the family with kidney failure , dialysis or transplant ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so let's get your ultrasound done , and we'll see how it goes .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right , that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . let me take a quick look at her .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . please use my physical exam template . um , i wan na take a quick listen to her heart and lungs . i'll look in her ears too . and she can sit , she can just sit on your lap .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . that's it .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right , that was n't too bad .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmm . so , let's complete the ultrasound today . i'll call you with the results . if it's normal , you wo n't need to see me again , but if it's abnormal , you can see me in kennesaw .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . we'll determine what the next steps are if there are any , after we see her results .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right , sounds good . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . the nurse will be in to have you complete some paperwork , and give you instructions for the ultrasound . we'll talk soon .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right . thank you , and have a good day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . physical exams show the well-nourished female , who is slightly fussy when examined . eyes are small appearing . she has mild hypotonia of the lower extremities in her arms . normal external female genitalia .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: assessment and plan . katherine is a 22-month-old former 34 and 3-week-old , twin with smith magenis syndrome . several organ systems can be affected by this chromosomal deletion syndrome . congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract have been reported in the literature .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we will obtain the screening of the kidneys by ultrasound today . if there are abnormalities on the kidney ultrasound , we will determine next steps and future follow-up . the family lives in dallas , georgia , so her follow-up should be at the town center location .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: end of recording .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 22-month-old female born at 34 weeks and 3 days gestation, who is a twin.
The patient has a 9-year-old brother diagnosed with autism.
The patient had a birth weight of 4 pounds 8 ounces.
The patient experienced difficulties with eating and breathing at birth.
The patient has a history of temperature regulation issues, severe reflux, and was diagnosed with Smith-Magenis syndrome by a neurologist.
The patient is currently in feeding, occupational, and physical therapy.
The patient will be starting speech therapy in the next few weeks.
The geneticist informed the mother that Smith-Magenis syndrome is associated with potential kidney fusion and leg issues.
Renal screening
The doctor asks about the number of wet diapers the patient has in a 24-hour period.
The patient has approximately 8 to 10 wet diapers in a 24-hour period.
The patient consumes Ripple Pea Protein milk instead of cow's milk.
The patient drinks water and water mixed with a small amount of juice.
The patient's twin sister is healthy with no known health conditions.
The patient and her twin sister are approximately the same size.
The doctor declares that a kidney ultrasound is necessary to screen for congenital anomalies.
The family lives in Dallas, Georgia.
The doctor asks if there is any family history of kidney failure, dialysis, or transplant.
There is no known family history of kidney failure, dialysis, or transplant.
The doctor performs a physical exam including listening to the heart and lungs and checking the ears.
The patient will complete a kidney ultrasound today, and the doctor will call with results.
Physical exam findings include a well-nourished female, microphthalmia, and mild hypotonia of the lower extremities and arms.
If the ultrasound is abnormal, follow-up will be scheduled at the Town Center location.
